slides.subheading: Learning precise folding with geometric shapes
slides.mascot_explanation: Hi friends! I'm Betsy Bear and today we're going to learn how to fold napkins into different shapes like triangles and rectangles. It's like magic how a square napkin can become so many different shapes!

items: 4-6 cloth napkins
small basket or container
flat work surface
damp cloth for cleanup
slides.setup_instructions: Arrange napkins flat on your work surface with the basket nearby for storing folded napkins.
slides.mascot_explanation: Let's gather our soft napkins and make sure we have a nice flat space to work. I love how the napkins feel so smooth in my paws! Having everything ready helps us focus on our folding.

slides.text: Take one napkin and lay it flat. Fold it in half to make a rectangle, pressing the edges to make them crisp and neat.
slides.mascot_explanation: Watch how I carefully bring one edge to meet the other edge perfectly. Press down gently with your hands to make a nice crisp fold - just like when I smooth down my fur!

slides.text: Now fold your rectangle in half again to make a smaller square, then try folding diagonally corner to corner to make a triangle.
slides.mascot_explanation: Look how the napkin keeps changing shapes! When we fold corner to corner, we make a triangle - it has three sides and three corners, just like the triangle on my favorite honey jar!

slides.prompt: Practice folding each napkin into rectangles, squares, and triangles. Take your time and make each fold neat and precise.
slides.mascot_explanation: Now it's your turn to be the folding expert! I'll watch as you make beautiful shapes with your napkins. Remember, there's no hurry - the most important thing is making neat, careful folds.

items: Can you fold a napkin into a rectangle?
What shape do you get when you fold corner to corner?
How many layers does your napkin have after folding it twice?
slides.mascot_explanation: Let's see what you've learned about folding! I'm so proud of how carefully you worked with each napkin. You're becoming a real folding expert like me!

slides.text: Place all folded napkins neatly in the basket and wipe your work surface clean. Notice how organized and beautiful your folded napkins look.
slides.mastery_check: Child can independently fold napkins into basic geometric shapes with neat, pressed edges.
slides.mascot_explanation: What beautiful folded napkins you made! They look so neat and tidy in the basket. Taking care of our materials and cleaning up is just as important as the folding itself.

title: Sandpaper Letters Review: Letters d, b, c, e, f with I Spy Games
description: Children review the sandpaper letters d, b, c, e, and f through tactile tracing and I Spy games in different positions. This reinforces letter recognition and phonetic understanding while building pre-writing skills.
instructions: 1. Gather sandpaper letters d, b, c, e, f and arrange them on a work mat in front of your child.
2. Review each letter by having your child trace with two fingers while saying the phonetic sound (not letter name).
3. Practice the three-period lesson: 'This is d' (trace), 'Show me d' (child finds), 'What is this?' (child names).
4. Play I Spy focusing on beginning sounds: 'I spy something that starts with /d/' around the room.
5. Extend to middle and ending positions: 'I spy something with /f/ at the end' (like 'roof').
6. Have your child close their eyes and trace letters in the air while you call out sounds.
7. Create simple words using known letters and have child identify individual sounds.
8. End by having child choose their favorite letter to trace three times while saying the sound.
materials_needed: sandpaper letters d, b, c, e, f
small objects around room for I Spy
parent_notes: The tactile experience of sandpaper letters engages multiple senses and strengthens neural pathways for letter recognition. Always use phonetic sounds rather than letter names at this stage. If your child confuses b and d, don't worry - this is developmentally normal and will resolve with continued practice. The I Spy games help children understand that sounds can appear in different positions within words, preparing them for reading and spelling.

slides.heading: Sandpaper Letters Review: Letters d, b, c, e, f with I Spy Games
slides.subheading: Tracing letters and finding sounds all around us
slides.mascot_explanation: Hello my clever cubs! It's Betsy Bear, and today we're going to trace our special sandpaper letters and play detective with sounds. We'll use our fingers to feel the letters and our ears to find sounds hiding everywhere!

items: sandpaper letters d, b, c, e, f
work mat or tray
small objects around room for I Spy
paper for air tracing (optional)
slides.setup_instructions: Place the sandpaper letters on your work mat in a row where your child can easily reach and trace them.
slides.mascot_explanation: Let's set up our special letters on the mat so you can feel their bumpy texture. I love how these letters feel under my paws - they help my fingers remember the shape while my brain remembers the sound!

slides.text: Trace each sandpaper letter with two fingers, following the correct formation while saying the phonetic sound (/d/, /b/, /c/, /e/, /f/).
slides.mascot_explanation: Use your pointer finger and middle finger together, just like I do with my claws! Start at the top and follow the bumpy path while making the sound. Feel how each letter has its own special shape and sound.

slides.text: Play I Spy with beginning sounds: 'I spy with my little eye something that starts with /d/.' Look around the room for objects that begin with each letter sound.
slides.mascot_explanation: Now we're sound detectives! I'll give you a clue with a sound, and you find something that starts with that sound. Maybe you'll find a 'door' for /d/ or a 'chair' for /c/!

slides.prompt: Continue I Spy games with middle and ending sounds, then practice air tracing while eyes are closed. Take turns being the I Spy leader.
slides.mascot_explanation: This is the really fun part! Close your eyes and trace letters in the air - it's like magic writing! Then you can be the teacher and give ME I Spy clues. I love when you teach me new things!

items: Can you trace each letter while saying its sound?
Can you find objects that start with these letter sounds?
What sound does this letter make? (point to random letter)
slides.mascot_explanation: Let me see how well you know these letters and sounds! You've been working so hard, and I can tell your fingers and brain are getting really good at remembering letters.

slides.text: Choose your favorite letter from today to trace three times while saying its sound clearly. Put letters away carefully in their proper place.
slides.mastery_check: Child can trace letters d, b, c, e, f with correct formation while producing accurate phonetic sounds and identify these sounds in different word positions.
slides.mascot_explanation: What a wonderful job you did today! Pick your favorite letter to trace one more time - that's like giving it a special goodbye hug. You're becoming such a great reader and writer!

title: Cards and Counters: Exploring Odd and Even Numbers 1-10
description: Children use number cards and counting objects to discover the concrete difference between odd and even numbers. This foundational concept helps develop number sense and prepares for later arithmetic operations.
instructions: 1. Set out number cards 1-10 in order and provide 55 small counting objects (buttons, beans, etc.).
2. Start with card 1 - have child count out 1 counter and place it below the card.
3. For card 2 - count out 2 counters and arrange them in a pair side by side, showing they 'match up'.
4. Continue with card 3 - arrange 3 counters trying to make pairs, noting that one is left over.
5. Work through all numbers 1-10, arranging counters in pairs and identifying which numbers have leftovers.
6. Introduce vocabulary: numbers that pair up completely are 'even', numbers with leftovers are 'odd'.
7. Go back through and have child identify each number as odd or even by looking at the counter arrangements.
8. Play a quick game: call out a number and have child predict if it's odd or even before checking with counters.
materials_needed: 55 small counting objects (buttons, beans, or blocks)
parent_notes: This concrete exploration of odd and even numbers builds deep understanding through manipulation and visual patterns. Children naturally begin to see that even numbers can be split into equal groups while odd numbers cannot. Don't rush to abstract rules - let your child discover the patterns through hands-on experience. This foundation will support later understanding of division, multiplication, and number relationships.

slides.heading: Cards and Counters: Exploring Odd and Even Numbers 1-10
slides.subheading: Discovering which numbers pair up perfectly and which have leftovers
slides.mascot_explanation: Hi there, number explorers! I'm Betsy Bear and today we're going to be math detectives. We'll use our number cards and counters to discover a special secret about numbers - some like to pair up perfectly, and others always have one left over!

items: number cards 1-10
55 small counting objects (buttons, beans, or blocks)
work mat
small bowl for counters
slides.setup_instructions: Arrange number cards 1-10 in order at the top of your work mat with the bowl of counters nearby.
slides.mascot_explanation: Let's set up our number cards in a nice neat row from 1 to 10. Look at all these counters we have - we'll need lots of them because we're going to count all the way up to 10! I love organizing everything before we start.

slides.text: Start with number card 1. Count out 1 counter and place it below the card. Try to make pairs - can you pair up all the counters?
slides.mascot_explanation: Let's start with just one counter under the number 1. Look at that lonely counter all by itself! It can't find a partner to pair up with, can it? That makes me think number 1 is special.

slides.text: Move to number card 2. Count out 2 counters and arrange them side by side in a pair. See how they match up perfectly with no leftovers?
slides.mascot_explanation: Now we have 2 counters, and look! They can be best friends and pair up perfectly, just like my two ears! When all the counters can find a partner, we call that number 'even' because everything is matched up evenly.

slides.prompt: Continue working through numbers 3-10, arranging counters in pairs under each number card and observing which numbers have leftovers and which pair up completely.
slides.mascot_explanation: Now for the exciting discovery time! Work through each number and try to make pairs. You'll notice something really cool - some numbers let all their counters find partners, while others always have one counter left out. I wonder what pattern you'll discover!

items: Which numbers from 1-10 have all their counters paired up?
Which numbers have one counter left over?
Is the number 8 odd or even? How do you know?
slides.mascot_explanation: Let's see what you discovered about our numbers! You've been such a careful observer. Can you tell me which numbers like to pair up completely and which ones always have a leftover? You're becoming a real number pattern expert!

slides.text: Review that even numbers (2,4,6,8,10) pair up completely while odd numbers (1,3,5,7,9) have one leftover. Clean up by counting materials back into containers.
slides.mastery_check: Child can arrange counters for numbers 1-10, identify the pairing pattern, and correctly classify numbers as odd or even based on the concrete arrangement.
slides.mascot_explanation: What an amazing discovery you made today! Even numbers are like perfectly matched pairs of socks, while odd numbers always have one extra. Now you know a special secret about numbers that will help you with lots of math adventures!

title: Tempera Brush Painting: Creating Color Patterns and Shapes
description: Children explore tempera paint techniques using brushes to create intentional patterns and geometric shapes. This lesson focuses on brush control, color mixing, and artistic expression while developing fine motor skills.
instructions: 1. Set up painting station with tempera paints (red, blue, yellow, white), brushes, water cups, and paper.
2. Demonstrate proper brush holding and paint loading - dip brush, tap excess on rim, stroke on paper.
3. Show how to clean brush between colors by swirling in water and dabbing on paper towel.
4. Practice making basic shapes: circles, triangles, and squares using brush strokes.
5. Explore creating patterns by repeating shapes or colors in sequences (red circle, blue square, red circle).
6. Encourage gentle color mixing by touching wet colors together on paper to see new colors appear.
7. Allow free exploration time for child to create their own painting using learned techniques.
8. Clean up together by washing brushes, wiping surfaces, and displaying finished artwork to dry.
parent_notes: Tempera painting develops hand strength, coordination, and artistic expression while introducing color theory concepts. Focus on the process rather than the final product - the exploration and technique practice are most important. Allow your child to discover color mixing naturally rather than directing outcomes. This medium is forgiving and encourages experimentation, building confidence in artistic abilities.

slides.heading: Tempera Brush Painting: Creating Color Patterns and Shapes
slides.subheading: Exploring paint techniques with brushes and discovering color mixing
slides.mascot_explanation: Hello my artistic friends! It's Betsy Bear, and today we're going to be painters! We'll use special brushes and colorful tempera paints to make beautiful shapes and patterns. I can't wait to see what magical colors you'll create!

items: tempera paints (red, blue, yellow, white)
medium brushes
painting paper
water cups
paper towels
aprons or old clothes
slides.setup_instructions: Cover work area with newspaper, set out paints in small containers, fill water cups halfway, and have paper towels ready for cleanup.
slides.mascot_explanation: Let's get ready to paint like real artists! Put on your apron so your clothes stay clean, just like I wear my painting smock. See all these beautiful colors waiting for us? We have everything we need to make wonderful art!

slides.text: Hold your brush like a pencil and dip it gently in paint. Tap the excess on the rim and practice making smooth brush strokes on your paper.
slides.mascot_explanation: Hold your brush gently but firmly, just like I hold my honey spoon! Dip it in the paint like you're dipping a cookie in milk - not too deep, and tap off the extra. Now make some practice strokes on your paper.

slides.text: Try painting basic shapes: circles, triangles, and squares. Clean your brush in water between colors and dab it on the paper towel.
slides.mascot_explanation: Let's paint some shapes! Make a round circle like the sun, a pointy triangle like a mountain, and a square like a window. When you want a new color, give your brush a little bath in the water and dry it on the paper towel.

slides.prompt: Create patterns using your shapes and colors, then explore what happens when wet colors touch each other. Paint freely and experiment with your brush techniques.
slides.mascot_explanation: Now comes the really fun part - you're the artist in charge! Try making patterns like red-blue-red-blue, or see what happens when your wet colors meet each other. It's like magic when colors blend together!

items: Can you paint a circle, triangle, and square?
What happens when you mix red and blue paint?
How do you clean your brush between colors?
slides.mascot_explanation: Look at all the beautiful art you've created! You've learned so much about painting today. Can you show me your favorite shape that you painted? I love seeing all the different colors you discovered!

slides.text: Clean brushes thoroughly in water, wipe your workspace, and set your painting in a safe place to dry. Look at all the beautiful colors and patterns you created!
slides.mastery_check: Child demonstrates proper brush handling, can create basic geometric shapes with paint, and understands brush cleaning procedures between colors.
slides.mascot_explanation: What a wonderful painting session we had! Your artwork is so colorful and creative. Let's clean up our brushes so they'll be ready for our next art adventure. I'm so proud of the beautiful art you made today!

title: The Tiny Seed by Eric Carle: Following Nature's Growth Cycle
description: Children listen to Eric Carle's story about a tiny seed's journey through seasons and challenges to become a giant flower. This story connects to science concepts while building listening comprehension and vocabulary.
instructions: 1. Gather comfortably with 'The Tiny Seed' and perhaps a real seed or small plant to show before reading.
2. Introduce the book by examining the cover and predicting what might happen to the tiny seed.
3. Read slowly, pausing to discuss Eric Carle's distinctive collage illustrations and bright colors.
4. Stop at key moments to discuss what dangers the seed faces and how it survives.
5. Emphasize vocabulary words like 'autumn wind,' 'survive,' 'sprout,' and 'bloom' with gestures or actions.
6. After reading, discuss the seasonal cycle shown in the book and connect to real plants children have observed.
7. Encourage children to retell the seed's journey using their own words and hand motions.
8. End by looking at any real plants nearby and discussing what seeds need to grow (water, soil, sun).
materials_needed: The Tiny Seed by Eric Carle
paper and crayons for follow-up drawing
parent_notes: Eric Carle's books combine beautiful art with rich scientific content that appeals to young children's curiosity about nature. This story provides excellent opportunities to discuss perseverance, growth, and seasonal changes. Encourage your child to notice the detailed collage illustrations and discuss how the seed overcomes challenges. The cyclical nature of the story helps children understand sequence and cause-and-effect relationships.

slides.heading: The Tiny Seed by Eric Carle: Following Nature's Growth Cycle
slides.subheading: Discovering how a small seed becomes a magnificent flower
slides.mascot_explanation: Hello my little seedlings! It's story time with Betsy Bear! Today we're reading about a brave little seed that goes on an amazing journey. Just like how I started as a tiny cub and grew big and strong, this seed has quite an adventure ahead!

items: The Tiny Seed by Eric Carle
real seed or small plant (optional)
comfortable seating area
paper and crayons for follow-up drawing
slides.setup_instructions: Find a cozy reading spot with good lighting and have the book ready to share. Keep any real plants or seeds nearby to show during discussion.
slides.mascot_explanation: Let's snuggle up in our cozy reading spot! I brought this beautiful book by Eric Carle - he's the same artist who made 'The Very Hungry Caterpillar'! Look at these gorgeous colors on the cover. Do you see that tiny little seed?

slides.text: Look at the book cover together and predict what might happen to the tiny seed. Discuss what seeds need to grow and where they come from.
slides.mascot_explanation: Before we start reading, let's be prediction detectives! What do you think will happen to this tiny seed? I wonder if it will grow into something beautiful, just like the acorns near my tree home grow into mighty oak trees!

slides.text: Read the story slowly, pausing to discuss Eric Carle's collage art and the dangers the seeds face during their journey with the autumn wind.
slides.mascot_explanation: Listen carefully to all the adventures these seeds have! Eric Carle makes such beautiful pictures by cutting and pasting colorful paper - just like the collages we make! Some seeds face scary challenges, but our tiny seed is very brave.

slides.prompt: After reading, retell the seed's journey together using hand motions. Act out being blown by wind, falling to earth, and growing tall like the giant flower.
slides.mascot_explanation: Now let's act out the story with our whole bodies! Pretend you're a tiny seed blowing in the wind, then slowly growing roots, sprouting up, and becoming a giant flower reaching for the sun. I love how you move like growing plants!

items: What did the seed need to grow big and strong?
What challenges did the seeds face on their journey?
How did the tiny seed become the biggest flower of all?
slides.mascot_explanation: You listened so carefully to our story! Can you tell me what helped our tiny seed grow so big and beautiful? You remembered all the important parts of the seed's amazing journey. What smart listeners you are!

slides.text: Discuss how this story connects to real plants and seeds you've seen. If desired, draw a picture showing the seed's journey from tiny seed to giant flower with all its seeds.
slides.mastery_check: Child can retell key events from the story, identify what seeds need to grow, and make connections between the story and real plants in their environment.
slides.mascot_explanation: What a wonderful story about growing and never giving up! Just like that brave little seed, you keep growing and learning new things every day. Maybe we can plant some real seeds and watch them grow, just like in our story!
